objective: Test whether static component-value-digest-spread candidate ordering improves the Wave 63 bounded selector bottleneck.
non_negotiables: Use the same Wave 62 capacity-clearing source and 2,500 candidate-pair budget as Wave 63.
Treat worse or limit-hit results as a selector-ordering no-go, not as source-capacity evidence.
Do not promote exact support from bounded selector diagnostics.
Route next work toward dynamic unseen-value pairing if static digest spread does not improve selected rows.

tasks.prompt: Add and run a value-digest-spread bounded selector ordering for the Wave 62 left-supply source.
acceptance: The report uses the same candidate-pair budget as Wave 63.
The report records selected rows, topology counts, limit-hit status, and rejection counts.
Docs compare selected-row count directly against Wave 63.

tasks.prompt: Record Wave 64 as a static value-spread selector no-go and route next work to dynamic unseen-value selection.
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs state that value-spread ordering selects 4 rows under the 2,500-pair bound.
Docs state that Wave 63's profile-index order selected 12 rows under the same bound.
Docs identify static digest-spread ordering as a no-go.
Docs identify dynamic unseen-value pairing or materialization-aware search as the next source-support gate.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_64_value_spread_selector_no_go.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 64 plan is internally consistent.
The Wave 64 value-spread selector report is valid JSON.
